_comment: OpenSSF Best Practices badge (passing level) proposed answers for Podspine. Read by bestpractices.dev when the project entry is edited / re-run with 'Save (and continue)'. Status values: Met | Unmet | N/A ('?' / unknown are ignored). Justifications are a starting point; the authorized maintainer reviews and confirms each before saving. Project: https://github.com/schubydoo/podspine
description_good_justification: The README and documentation site describe what Podspine does (turns audiobook files into per-chapter podcast RSS feeds any podcatcher can play) up front. https://github.com/schubydoo/podspine
interact_justification: Users can ask questions and interact via GitHub Issues: https://github.com/schubydoo/podspine/issues
contribution_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md at the repository root explains how to contribute: https://github.com/schubydoo/podspine/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
contribution_requirements_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md documents the requirements for contributions: conventional-commit PR titles, passing CI (cargo fmt, clippy -D warnings, tests), and changeset entries. https://github.com/schubydoo/podspine/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
floss_license_justification: Released under the GNU AGPL-3.0, an OSI-approved and FSF-recognized FLOSS license.
license_location_justification: The license is in the standard LICENSE file at the repository root: https://github.com/schubydoo/podspine/blob/main/LICENSE
documentation_basics_justification: The README plus a full documentation site cover installation, configuration, and usage: https://schubydoo.github.io/podspine/
documentation_interface_justification: The docs document the external interface: the HTTP/RSS feed endpoints, configuration flags, and deployment. https://schubydoo.github.io/podspine/
sites_https_justification: The project repository (github.com) and documentation site (schubydoo.github.io) are served exclusively over HTTPS.
discussion_justification: GitHub Issues and pull requests provide discussion of proposed changes and issues that is searchable, allows messages/topics to be addressed by URL, and does not require client-side JavaScript. https://github.com/schubydoo/podspine/issues
english_justification: All documentation and project communication are in English.
maintained_justification: The project is actively maintained; changes ship regularly through reviewed pull requests (latest release v1.2.0).
repo_public_justification: The source is in a public git repository: https://github.com/schubydoo/podspine
repo_track_justification: git records what changes were made, who made them, and when.
repo_interim_justification: Interim development versions are publicly available between releases as commits on the main branch.
repo_distributed_justification: The project uses git, a distributed version control system.
version_unique_justification: Each release has a unique version identifier, set in Cargo.toml and the git tag (e.g. v1.2.0).
version_tags_justification: Every release is a git tag of the form vX.Y.Z.
release_notes_justification: Each release has human-readable notes in CHANGELOG.md and on the GitHub Releases page. https://github.com/schubydoo/podspine/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
release_notes_vulns_justification: No publicly known vulnerabilities have been fixed to date; the changelog reserves a dedicated `security` category (via knope changesets) for such fixes when they occur.
report_process_justification: The process for reporting bugs (GitHub Issues) is documented in CONTRIBUTING.md and the issue tracker: https://github.com/schubydoo/podspine/issues
report_tracker_justification: Bugs are tracked in GitHub Issues.
report_responses_justification: The maintainer responds to bug reports filed as GitHub Issues.
enhancement_responses_justification: Enhancement requests filed as GitHub Issues receive responses, including a decision when a request is declined.
report_archive_justification: The bug report history is publicly archived and searchable in GitHub Issues: https://github.com/schubydoo/podspine/issues
vulnerability_report_process_justificati: SECURITY.md documents how to report vulnerabilities: https://github.com/schubydoo/podspine/blob/main/SECURITY.md
vulnerability_report_private_justificati: Vulnerabilities can be reported privately via the contact in SECURITY.md and via GitHub private security advisories: https://github.com/schubydoo/podspine/security/advisories
vulnerability_report_response_justificat: SECURITY.md commits to acknowledging vulnerability reports within a stated timeframe.
build_justification: The project builds from source with `cargo build`.
build_common_tools_justification: It is built with Cargo, the standard and widely available Rust build tool.
build_floss_tools_justification: The build uses only FLOSS tools (cargo, rustc).
test_justification: An automated test suite runs with `cargo test` (workspace-wide, ~90% line coverage).
test_invocation_justification: Tests are run via the standard `cargo test`, documented in the contributing/development docs.
test_most_justification: The test suite covers most of the code (~90% line coverage, enforced by a coverage floor).
test_continuous_integration_justificatio: CI runs the full test suite on every pull request and push (GitHub Actions).
test_policy_justification: Project policy requires tests for major new functionality; this is documented in the contributing docs and enforced by the coverage floor.
tests_are_added_justification: New functionality is accompanied by tests; the CI coverage floor (90%) enforces this on every PR.
tests_documented_added_justification: The policy of adding tests with new functionality is documented in the contributing/development docs.
warnings_justification: Compiler and linter warnings are enabled (cargo build warnings plus clippy).
warnings_fixed_justification: Warnings are treated as errors: CI runs `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, so code with warnings cannot merge.
warnings_strict_justification: The project builds with maximally strict warnings: clippy with `-D warnings` across the full workspace.
know_secure_design_justification: The design applies secure-design principles: least privilege, input validation, canonicalized path resolution confined to the library root, and no shell interpolation of untrusted input. These are documented and enforced in code review.
know_common_errors_justification: Implemented in Rust (memory-safe by default); the known-risk areas — path traversal in id-to-path resolution and command injection in ffmpeg arguments — are explicitly guarded (argv vectors, never shell strings) and audited.
crypto_published_justification: The only cryptographic use is capability-URL token generation via the OS CSPRNG (getrandom) and BLAKE3 for stable identifiers — all publicly published, expert-reviewed algorithms. No bespoke cryptography.
crypto_call_justification: Cryptographic and random functionality is delegated to established FLOSS crates (getrandom, blake3); nothing is reimplemented.
crypto_floss_justification: All cryptographic dependencies (getrandom, blake3) are FLOSS.
crypto_keylength_justification: Podspine uses no cryptographic keys; capability tokens are at least 128-bit random values, not keys.
crypto_working_justification: No broken cryptographic algorithms (e.g. MD5/SHA-1 for security, single DES, RC4, Dual_EC_DRBG) are used.
crypto_weaknesses_justification: No cryptographic hash with known collision weaknesses is used for security purposes.
crypto_pfs_justification: Podspine terminates no TLS itself; transport security (and perfect forward secrecy) is provided by the operator's reverse proxy.
crypto_password_storage_justification: Podspine stores no user passwords; it has no user accounts. Access is granted through unguessable capability URLs.
crypto_random_justification: Security-sensitive capability tokens are generated with a cryptographically secure PRNG (the OS getrandom source).
delivery_mitm_justification: Releases are distributed over HTTPS with SHA-256 checksums and cosign/Sigstore signatures plus SLSA provenance.
delivery_unsigned_justification: All documented install methods use HTTPS and verify a SHA-256 checksum; release artifacts are cryptographically signed.
vulnerabilities_fixed_60_days_justificat: There are no known unpatched vulnerabilities; dependencies are monitored with cargo audit, cargo deny, Renovate, and Dependabot and updated promptly.
vulnerabilities_critical_fixed_justifica: No unpatched critical vulnerabilities exist; supply-chain and code scanning run in CI.
no_leaked_credentials_justification: No valid credentials are committed to the repository; gitleaks scans every push and GitHub secret scanning is enabled.
static_analysis_justification: Static analysis runs in CI: clippy (`-D warnings`), CodeQL, cargo audit, cargo deny, zizmor, and OpenSSF Scorecard.
static_analysis_common_vulnerabilities_j: CodeQL and cargo-audit look for common vulnerability classes.
static_analysis_fixed_justification: Medium-and-higher static-analysis findings are fixed before merge; clippy warnings are hard errors in CI.
static_analysis_often_justification: Static analysis runs on every pull request and push, plus scheduled Scorecard and CodeQL runs.
dynamic_analysis_unsafe_justification: Fuzz targets build with sanitizers (AddressSanitizer) to detect memory-safety issues.
dynamic_analysis_enable_assertions_justi: Fuzz builds run with debug assertions enabled.
dynamic_analysis_fixed_justification: There are no outstanding fuzzing-discovered defects; crashes found by fuzzing are fixed and the corpus is retained.
